body.title: Granny: _cc0_busy() misses in_progress tickets with worker=None
body.result: _setstatus_direct() now sets worker='claude' on CC.0 dispatch; _cc0_busy() correctly detects CC busy
body.intention: I intend that _cc0_busy() correctly detects CC.0 as busy after dispatch.
body.description:
_dispatch_cc0() marks tickets in_progress via _setstatus_direct() but never sets the worker field. _cc0_busy() queries for worker IN ('claude','cc') and misses them. Result: Granny dispatches a new ticket to CC.0 on the next 60s cycle even though CC is already sprinting. Fix: extend _setstatus_direct() to optionally set worker field; call with worker='claude' in _dispatch_cc0().

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py (_setstatus_direct, _dispatch_cc0)
**Design rules:** none apply — query fix
**Scope boundary:** Only _setstatus_direct and _dispatch_cc0. Out of scope: changing _cc0_busy query structure.
**Completion criteria:** After _dispatch_cc0() runs, the dispatched ticket has worker='claude' in the DB; _cc0_busy() returns True on next poll.
